resources.name: E-tečaj A1.hr (University of Zagreb)
resources.why: The official free A1 e-course from Croaticum (University of Zagreb): 80 units of ~45 minutes, each with listening, pronunciation, writing and comprehension. Your main input source in Phase 1 — the app schedules it unit by unit.

resources.name: E-tečaj A2.hr (University of Zagreb)
resources.why: The official A2 continuation: 80 more units in the same listening-first format. Your main input source in Phase 2 — scheduled by the app so there is never a question of what to do next.

resources.name: Croaticum — official exams & sample tests
resources.why: Where the real exam happens: Croaticum administers the official Croatian language exams (B1 certificate for residence/citizenship), publishes the sample tasks the app's mock exams mirror, and takes bookings (contact ~10 days ahead with ID).

resources.name: Razgovarajte s nama! (Croaticum textbook series)
resources.why: The official University of Zagreb textbook line, used in Croaticum's own classrooms. The A2-B1 volume is the structured input source for Phase 3, when the free e-courses end.

resources.name: HRT news & podcasts (native media)
resources.why: Not casual listening — targeted exam training: the official exam's listening section is news-bulletin Croatian (traffic reports, announcements — see the Croaticum sample), and HRT is the national broadcaster producing exactly that format daily. Used in Phase 3/B1 only.

resources.name: Conversation practice (partner or tutor)
resources.why: The output half of learning: regular spoken conversation with a native speaker — a Croatian partner at home is the strongest asset a learner can have. The plan schedules these sessions; real dialogue does what no app can.
